summary: The erdos_problems dataset tracks 1,217 problems (1,179 open) curated by Terence Tao. Current activity is concentrated in two fully-resolved problems (erdos_0001, erdos_0002), each with one closed proof issue, while the proof-status quarters q1–q10 each carry exactly 100 issues, suggesting an evenly distributed but largely unprocessed backlog. There is no evidence yet of partial-progress clustering, indicating the verification pipeline is at an early stage.
problems: erdos_0001 and erdos_0002 are the only problems with attached proof issues, and both are already fully resolved with untitled proofs that lack descriptive metadata.
Proof status buckets q1 through q10 are uniformly loaded at 100 issues each, an unusually flat distribution that points to bulk import rather than organic, problem-driven activity.
The two resolved proofs carry generic 'Proof of erdos_NNNN: (untitled)' labels, providing no abstract, technique tags, or reviewer notes for downstream reuse.
The vast majority of the 1,179 open problems have zero issues, so traction signals are effectively absent and prioritization cannot yet be data-driven.
highlights: Two problems (erdos_0001, erdos_0002) have completed proofs with closed issues, demonstrating the resolution workflow functions end to end.
A balanced 100-issue load across all ten q-buckets means workload can be parallelized cleanly across reviewers with no hotspot bottleneck.
Rich dataset metadata (cash prizes, tags, OEIS references, status) is available to enrich currently-untitled proofs and to seed automated prioritization.
Maintenance by Terence Tao gives high authoritative confidence in problem statements and resolution status.

suggested_todos.title: Backfill titles, abstracts, and technique tags onto the two resolved proofs (erdos_0001, erdos_0002) so they are reusable references
Sample and triage 10 issues from each q1–q10 bucket to detect whether the flat 100-issue distribution hides near-solvable problems
Cross-reference open problems against OEIS links and cash-prize tags to build a candidate shortlist of likely-solvable-soon problems
Define a 'partial progress' status label so the next reporting cycle can surface traction beyond binary open/resolved
Audit the bulk-imported issues to confirm they map to real problems and remove duplicate or placeholder entries
